Content_Sketch: News calls it digital gold - you sit in a cafe staring at your coffee knowing you built it
Illustration_Note: Stickman in grey t-shirt at cafe table with laptop and coffee mug - TV screen in background showing Bitcoin news

Content_Sketch: 1 million coins sitting untouched - worth tens of billions - the largest fortune no one has ever touched
Illustration_Note: Bold text on dark: one million coins - never touched - the largest unclaimed fortune in history

Content_Sketch: You created digital gold - then you stood up and walked away
Illustration_Note: Bold text on dark: You created digital gold - then you walked away

Content_Sketch: Late nights coding alone - nobody asked you to fix money - but you cannot stop thinking about it
Illustration_Note: Stickman in dark hoodie at desk with multiple monitors - late night - code on screens - coffee mug beside keyboard

Content_Sketch: If you fail nobody knows - if you succeed everything changes - the weight of invisible stakes
Illustration_Note: Close-up of stickman hands on keyboard - screen glow on face - dark room around

Content_Sketch: Write 9-page whitepaper on Halloween night - send to cryptography mailing list - mostly silence
Illustration_Note: Stickman at desk - document on screen - email sending animation - empty inbox responses

Content_Sketch: Register bitcoin.org under fake name - create P2P Foundation profile with symbolic birthday April 5 1975
Illustration_Note: Stickman typing at desk - registration form on screen - fake identity details visible

Content_Sketch: You live alone with this secret - no colleagues no mentor - you choose anonymity because this must be bigger than any name
Illustration_Note: Stickman standing at window at night - dark room behind - city lights outside - completely alone

Content_Sketch: January 3 2009 - mine Genesis Block - embed newspaper headline about bank bailouts into the first block ever created
Illustration_Note: Stickman at desk - screen showing Block 0 mining - newspaper headline visible on desk

Content_Sketch: Send 10 BTC to Hal Finney - first transaction in history - worth exactly nothing
Illustration_Note: Stickman at desk sending transaction - faceless tall stickman (Finney) visible on split screen receiving

Content_Sketch: People start using it - not because they trust you - because they trust the system - that is what you wanted - and what will erase you
Illustration_Note: Multiple faceless stickmen at various computers - network growing - subject stickman watching from corner

Content_Sketch: 10000 BTC buys 2 pizzas - nobody realizes they are holding gold yet
Illustration_Note: Split scene - pizza delivery on one side - gold bars equivalent on other side - contrast

Content_Sketch: WikiLeaks accepts Bitcoin - you warn the community about kicking a hornet nest too early
Illustration_Note: Stickman at desk typing warning message on forum - news headlines about WikiLeaks visible on second monitor

Content_Sketch: You begin to understand - either you leave or you destroy it - the project is only decentralized when its creator walks away
Illustration_Note: Close-up of stickman face - dot eyes staring at screen showing growing network graph - realization moment

Content_Sketch: Hand over source code and network alert key to Gavin Andresen - the transfer of everything
Illustration_Note: Two stickmen at virtual handoff - subject passing folder/key icon to faceless successor stickman

Content_Sketch: April 23 email to Mike Hearn - I have moved on to other things - it is in good hands
Illustration_Note: Stickman typing final emails - screen showing sent messages - each one a goodbye

Content_Sketch: April 26 final email to Gavin - stop calling me a mysterious shadowy figure - April 27 Gavin announces CIA presentation
Illustration_Note: Stickman reading forum post about CIA - screen glow on face - stillness

Content_Sketch: You sit there a little longer than usual with the thing you are about to leave behind - then close the laptop and walk away
Illustration_Note: Stickman hands on closed laptop - pushing chair back - standing up - walking away from desk into dark doorway

Content_Sketch: Bitcoin price climbs from 1 dollar to 100 to 1000 to 10000 to 60000 - digital gold becomes real - 1 million BTC equals tens of billions
Illustration_Note: Price ticker montage - numbers climbing - stickman in grey t-shirt watching from cafe - laptop showing price

Content_Sketch: The world keeps trying to find you - every few years someone becomes you - Dorian ambushed by reporters - Craig Wright rejected by court - you let them
Illustration_Note: Montage - faceless wrong-man stickman surrounded by press - faceless claimant in courtroom - subject watching TV from distance

Content_Sketch: Hal Finney dies 2014 - the first person who believed in you - you cannot attend his funeral
Illustration_Note: Stickman in grey t-shirt alone at cafe table - news on laptop screen - unable to attend - grief in isolation

Content_Sketch: Digital gold is everywhere - the person who created it is nowhere - you sit in a cafe and someone says Bitcoin is the future and you nod politely
Illustration_Note: Stickman at cafe - faceless stranger talking about Bitcoin at next table - subject nodding - invisible creator among believers

Content_Sketch: Cafe - old laptop - Bitcoin price on screen - stranger asks where Satoshi is now - you take a sip of coffee and say nothing
Illustration_Note: Mirror of HOOK - same cafe - same laptop - same coffee - stranger speaking - subject silent

Content_Sketch: The price of creating digital gold is that you can never claim it as yours
Illustration_Note: Bold text on dark: The price of creating digital gold - is that you can never claim it as yours
